# SDK Reference

The `@glubean/sdk` package provides the core primitives for writing API tests.

## Installation

```bash
# deno.json is generated by glubean init
# The import map maps @glubean/sdk to the correct JSR package
```

## `test()`

The primary function for defining a test.

```typescript
import { test, expect } from "@glubean/sdk";

export const getUser = test("get-user", async (ctx) => {
  const res = await ctx.http.get("https://api.example.com/user/1");

  expect(res.status).toBe(200);
  expect(res.json()).toHaveProperty("role", "admin");
});
```

## `TestContext`

Every test receives a `ctx` object with the following methods:

### `ctx.http`

HTTP client for making requests. All requests are automatically traced.

```typescript
ctx.http.get(url, options?)
ctx.http.post(url, options?)
ctx.http.put(url, options?)
ctx.http.patch(url, options?)
ctx.http.delete(url, options?)
```

### `ctx.vars`

Access variables from your `.env` file.

```typescript
const baseUrl = ctx.vars.require("BASE_URL");
```

### `ctx.secrets`

Access secrets from your `.env.secrets` file. Values are masked in traces.

```typescript
const token = ctx.secrets.require("API_TOKEN");
```

### `ctx.log()`

Log messages that appear in the trace viewer.

```typescript
ctx.log("User created", { id: 123 });
```
